q: What is the Power Apps component framework (PCF) used to build?
o: Reusable code components that extend app UI beyond standard controls
why: PCF builds reusable code components (custom controls) for model-driven and canvas apps.

q: A key ADVANTAGE of PCF over HTML web resources is that components...
o: Bind to data/properties and are reusable and configurable across apps
why: PCF components are data-bound, configurable and reusable, unlike ad-hoc web resources.

q: Decide whether each PCF statement is true.

rows.s: PCF components can be used in both model-driven and canvas apps.
Creator Kit and community components can accelerate building UIs.
PCF is intended for server-side transactions.

why: PCF is client-side UI; usable in both app types; Creator Kit/community components help.

q: Which types of components can you create with PCF?
why: PCF supports field (single value) and dataset (grid/collection) components.

q: Which CLI tool scaffolds and builds PCF projects?
why: The Power Platform CLI (pac pcf init, then build) scaffolds and builds components.

q: Which IDE is commonly used to develop PCF components with the CLI?
why: VS Code (with the CLI/Node tooling) is the typical PCF development environment.

q: Decide whether each tooling statement is true.

rows.s: Node.js/npm are used to build PCF components.
The CLI can create a solution to package the component.

why: PCF builds rely on Node/npm; pac solution packages the component for deployment.

q: The PCF component lifecycle methods include which sequence?
why: PCF implements init (setup), updateView (render on change), getOutputs (return values) and destroy (cleanup).

q: Where are a component's inputs, outputs and resources declared?
why: The manifest declares properties (inputs/outputs), types and resource files.

q: Which manifest concept defines the data a field component is bound to?
o: A property (of a specific data type)
why: Manifest properties define bound values and their types for the component.

q: Decide whether each manifest/property statement is true.

rows.s: Properties can be input, output or bound.
The manifest specifies each property's data type.

why: Manifest properties declare direction/binding and data types for the component.

q: When a bound value changes, which lifecycle method is called to re-render?
why: updateView runs whenever data/context changes so the component re-renders.

q: Which statement best captures why teams choose PCF over ad-hoc scripting?
o: Reusability, typed properties and a defined lifecycle make components maintainable
why: PCF's typed properties, lifecycle and reusability yield maintainable, shareable UI.
